meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging Mastery: Portfolio Building for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn to build high-impact portfolios using advanced tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your engineering career.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a hurdle; it's a diagnostic skill. By shifting your focus toward building modular, portfolio-ready AI agents, you transform 'can't debug' moments into architectural refactoring opportunities. Use Antigravity to map your logic and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, turning complex errors into documented case studies for your professional portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a cryptic stack trace, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic, mocking cadence. You are hours deep into a refactor, and the logic that felt bulletproof at 2 AM is now a labyrinth of undefined variables and silent failures. This is the crucible of the modern AI engineer; the moment where most students quit, but where the top 1% find their breakthrough.

Transitioning from a coder who 'can't debug' to a lead engineer is not about memorizing syntax—it is about architectural discipline. By adopting a portfolio-first mindset, you stop treating errors as failures and start treating them as data points for your next project. It is time to stop wrestling with the code and start mastering the systems that run it.

steps.title: Define the System Architecture
steps.description: Before fixing bugs, visualize your data flow. Map out how your AI agent handles state and memory to identify where the logic breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase structure. Identify potential bottlenecks in state management and suggest a modular architecture that makes debugging easier.

steps.title: Automate Error Tracing
steps.description: Stop reading logs manually. Use Claude Code to inject instrumentation that captures the exact state of your variables before a crash occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated logging wrapper for my core functions that exports stack traces to a JSON file for analysis.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Coding Patterns
steps.description: Build robust error boundaries. By wrapping API calls in resilient patterns, you ensure your app remains stable even when external AI models fail.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my API integration to include retries, exponential backoff, and circuit breakers.

steps.title: Document the 'Why' for Your Portfolio
steps.description: Every bug you fix is a story. Document the technical challenge, your hypothesis, and the final architectural solution to showcase your problem-solving skills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize this bug fix as a technical case study for my developer portfolio, focusing on the architectural trade-offs made.

steps.title: Validate with Test-Driven Development
steps.description: Finalize your fix by writing a test suite that prevents the bug from recurring. This signals to employers that you value production-grade code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for this module, covering edge cases and potential failure modes.

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a feature request from your future self for better documentation.
Use Claude Code to perform 'Rubber Ducking' by explaining your code logic to the CLI agent before asking for a fix.
Always maintain a 'Debug Log' in your repository root; it serves as a roadmap of your growth for potential hiring managers.
Focus on state persistence; most bugs in AI apps are simply forgotten context or lost state variables.
Leverage Antigravity to compare your broken code against industry-standard boilerplates to spot structural discrepancies.
resources: Advanced Debugging Patterns in AI: https://github.com/awesome-ai-engineering
content.next_steps: Now that you have automated your debugging workflow, push your first 'Case Study' commit to your GitHub profile and start building your next AI-native feature.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to stop guessing and start building. Don't let a missing semicolon define your career trajectory. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
